essence: Mid-migration off the dead-screen MacBook Pro, David hits the macOS 'screen sharing is controlled by Remote Management' message blocking bidirectional MBP-to-M4 remote sessions and wants the remote-access troubleshooting captured in Ansible/machine-control documentation.

actions.text: Document remote access + Screen Sharing vs Remote Management troubleshooting for MBP-M4 bidirectional sessions

essence: David asks the agent to verify whether a git-committed folder actually landed on the M4 and to copy it over one more time during the emergency migration.

actions.text: Verify the committed folder is on the M4 and re-copy it

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: Reviewing the repo-audit folder view, David says the display is fine but two fields are missing: the absolute file path and the git remote information.

actions.text: Add absolute file path and remote info to the folder view

essence: Debugging the JPC conversion export plugin one issue at a time, David directs cleanup of DS_Store noise, asks for other noise-only concepts to be pre-emptively added to the audit system, and wants a list of files to add to git config on the other computer.

actions.text: Check and resolve the DS_Store noise issue and add pre-emptive noise concepts to the audit system
Produce the list of files to add to git config on the other computer

essence: With ~100GB copied during the migration, David asks for a du-based scan of the top five disk-consuming folders to find low-hanging-fruit deletions without risking important data.

actions.text: Run du to identify top 5 disk-consuming folders and flag safe low-value deletions

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: David and Joy meet a local designer about a mural/graphic for the shop, ask for portfolio samples and a price estimate, and agree to reconvene next Tuesday or Wednesday to decide.

actions.text: Reconvene with the designer next week (Tue/Wed) to decide mural design and price

essence: David corrects the repo audit's scope — it should only locate where repositories are and be rerunnable anytime, not report last-updated dates — and separately wants his DTV VC group message reframed to open with the actual question rather than mixed-in research.

actions.text: Fix repo audit to report repo locations only, verify the code's accuracy, and make it rerunnable
Reframe the DTV VC group message to lead with the 'has anyone done DTV recently with a problem' question

essence: Troubleshooting command differences on the M4 over SSH mid-migration, David asks the agent to verify whether shell config files (.zshrc, profile, bash) were moved off the dying laptop, fact-check a Stan Moore statement online, and help frame a DTV VC group question combining his concern with Stan's response.

actions.text: Verify whether .zshrc/profile/bash shell config files were transferred off the failing MacBook Pro
Frame a DTV VC group question combining David's original question with Stan Moore's response

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: Setting up dev tooling on the replacement machine, David tests SSH access, wrestles a fresh VS Code install to remove a 'build with agent' component he never installed, and asks for a report of how many components changed since last time.

actions.text: Remove/disable the 'build with agent' component from the fresh VS Code install

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: David decides the migration needs a maintained copied-vs-uncopied tracking file plus a second machine-readable version to drive a folder-level gap analysis, with key environment files included where folders aren't granular enough.

actions.text: Update the existing copied/uncopied repository tracking file
Create a machine-readable twin of the tracking file to support gap analysis

essence: Triaging repos during the emergency migration, David decides to copy over the Claude site + voice agent and repo-audit repos, delete projects he considers dead, drop the historical folder except Canva (only if it holds images), and get uncommitted/no-remote repos onto GitHub in addition to raw file copies.

actions.text: Spin up a background agent to gap-analyse non-dev folders that were never requested for transfer
Copy the Claude site + voice agent and repo-audit repos to the new machine
Remove dead repos and the historical folder (keep Canva only if it contains images)

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: David sets the emergency transfer priority — video projects folder first, then the whole dev directory in his given order — run asynchronously via one-at-a-time background agents, plus a quick scan to move secrets and environment configs early.

actions.text: Copy the video projects folder first, then the full dev directory in priority order via background agents
Scan for and quickly transfer secrets and environment config files

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: David's MacBook Pro screen dies after a reboot mid-Signal Studio work, wiping his terminal sessions; with only the two external screens still visible, he declares an emergency and decides to migrate everything off the laptop to the M4 over SSH.

actions.text: Migrate everything from the failing MacBook Pro to the M4 over SSH

loop.label: MBP screen death → M4 emergency migration

essence: David advises on a shop location's viability — the market's price tolerance there is unknown, and profitability must account for electricity costs and thrown-away food — before his screen problem interrupts.
